name: azure-monitor-ingestion-java
description: Azure Monitor Ingestion SDK for Java. Send custom logs to Azure Monitor via Data Collection Rules (DCR) and Data Collection Endpoints (DCE).
capabilities: Generate Java code to create LogsIngestionClient with DefaultAzureCredential
Upload custom log entries to Azure Monitor via Data Collection Endpoint
Configure concurrent uploads for large log collections
Handle partial upload failures with error consumers
Create synchronous and asynchronous client implementations
Structure log entries with proper schema for DCR transformation
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill containing usage examples for the official Azure Monitor Ingestion SDK for Java. No executable code, no security risks detected. Static analysis found 0 files with 0 suspicious patterns. The skill provides legitimate Azure SDK integration patterns with proper authentication using DefaultAzureCredential.
valueStatement: Custom application logs need centralized monitoring in Azure. This skill helps you ingest logs to Azure Monitor using Data Collection Rules and the official Java SDK.
userTitle: Send Custom Logs to Azure Monitor with Java
limitations: Requires existing Data Collection Endpoint and Data Collection Rule setup
Does not query logs - use azure-monitor-query skill for log queries
Only supports Azure Monitor ingestion API, not other logging destinations
Log schema must match DCR transformation rules before ingestion

useCases.targetUser: Backend Java developers building cloud-native applications
useCases.title: Application Telemetry Ingestion
useCases.description: Send custom application logs, metrics, and events from Java applications to Azure Monitor for centralized monitoring and alerting.

useCases.targetUser: Security engineers implementing audit trail requirements
useCases.title: Security Event Collection
useCases.description: Ingest security audit logs and compliance events to Azure Monitor SecurityEvents or CommonSecurityLog tables for SIEM integration.

useCases.targetUser: DevOps engineers monitoring business process health
useCases.title: Custom Business Metrics
useCases.description: Track business-specific events and KPIs by uploading structured logs to custom tables in Log Analytics workspace.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Client Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a synchronous LogsIngestionClient for Azure Monitor using environment variables for endpoint, DCR ID, and stream name. Include DefaultAzureCredential authentication.

promptTemplates.title: Upload Logs with Error Handling
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate Java code to upload a list of custom log entries to Azure Monitor with partial failure handling. Log failed entries without aborting the entire batch.

promptTemplates.title: Concurrent Large Upload
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an async upload method that sends 10000 log entries to Azure Monitor with maxConcurrency set to 5. Use Reactor Mono for reactive streaming.

promptTemplates.title: Custom Log Entry Model
promptTemplates.prompt: Define a Java class for a custom log entry with timeGenerated, severity, source, and message fields. Include getters for JSON serialization required by the Logs Ingestion API.

bestPractices: Batch log entries before uploading instead of sending one at a time for better performance
Set maxConcurrency option when uploading large log collections to improve throughput
Include TimeGenerated field in log entries as most Azure Monitor tables require timestamps
antiPatterns: Creating new client instances for each upload - reuse a single client throughout application lifecycle
Uploading logs synchronously in high-throughput scenarios - use async client for reactive patterns
Ignoring partial upload errors - always configure error consumer to handle failed entries

faq.question: What prerequisites are needed before using this skill?
faq.answer: You need a Data Collection Endpoint (DCE), Data Collection Rule (DCR), Log Analytics workspace, and a target table configured in Azure Monitor.

faq.question: How do I authenticate with Azure Monitor?
faq.answer: Use DefaultAzureCredential which supports managed identity, environment credentials, and other Azure authentication methods automatically.

faq.question: Can I upload logs to custom tables?
faq.answer: Yes, you can upload to custom tables using the Custom- prefix in the stream name, or to built-in tables like CommonSecurityLog and SecurityEvents.

faq.question: How do I handle upload failures?
faq.answer: Use LogsUploadOptions with setLogsUploadErrorConsumer to handle partial failures gracefully without aborting the entire batch.

faq.question: What is the difference between sync and async clients?
faq.answer: Synchronous client blocks until upload completes. Async client returns Mono<Void> for non-blocking reactive patterns with better throughput.

faq.question: How do I query the logs after ingestion?
faq.answer: Use the azure-monitor-query skill with LogsQueryClient to run KQL queries against your Log Analytics workspace.
